title: Creamy Norwegian Lentil Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Norwegian stew made with French lentils and a hint of coconut cream, perfect for a cold winter's night. This dish is kosher-friendly and features a blend of herbal flavors.
instructions: In a large pot, saute the chopped onion, carrots, and celery in a little bit of oil until the vegetables are tender.
Add the rinsed lentils, vegetable broth, thyme, bay leaves, salt, and pepper to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Stir in the coconut cream and continue to simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the stew has thickened slightly.
Remove the bay leaves and serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Let the stew cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
Reheat the stew gently over low heat, whisking constantly, until warmed through.
